author: 13omb3r
content: @DominicVain got me thinking yesterday: there should be some sort of bonus for the length of time a position is defended. The way we were speaking about it would likely disincentivize players from defending if the reward was much lower than simply “attacking”.  That way there could be significant upside to holding down positions. Not sure how to balance that but came to mind.

author: 𝕘𝕣𝕚𝕤𝕥
content:
I’m not sure some are realizing what this could display.

Search up “Star Citizen players per server.” 

Think of playing Apex Legends. 60 players are all on the same server. The same instance. Fortnite has 100. BF2042 has 124. War Thunder….32.

Think how difficult it is to have 60 players drop on a map in Apex. My squadmates can be in Japan. A team that rolls up can have a player from Scotland and another from Kansas.

Ping may be an issue, but there is no geolock. Each player feels like they are in the same world. Each player can interact with the other. 

There is a massive mountain that needs to be scaled for the game we all want to be brought to life.Forget the latency problem, or the gpu/cpu problem—> the server problem is the one I obsess on the most.

Why? Because it won’t feel immersive if a fleet battle is capped at 50 players per server.

This alpha is the one that has meant the most to me so far. I’ve bugged them so much about another project because I feel it is the single most important roadblock for any web3 game to overcome.

Eve is lauded for huge battles…thousands of players in one instance. Except the servers are melted. Slowed to a crawl.

To bring the possibility of even 1000 players per instance with the fidelity of UE5 to market ? Just the possibility of this existing in 5 years? It’s sublime. It’s simply beautiful.

And if the world is persistent? Then all bets are off…no way to imagine the impact of such a game.

1000 players battle for an asteroid. Your ship crashes on the asteroid. The battle lasts for hours. And days later a salvage ship discovers your wreck and begins to salvage for profit. Or…you drop your sidearm at a space station….and someone picks it up days later.

The team continues to allow me to dream that we have a chance for all of this. I don’t care if it’s at a smaller scale at first. I don’t care there’s only a 10% chance. That 10% contains a mirror world based in the far future. That 10% has an infinite horizon.

author: ROME | Kalvin KideanONI
content: I’m not sure some are realizing what this could display.  Search up “Star Citizen players per server.”   Think of playing Apex Legends. 60 players are all on the same server. The same instance. Fortnite has 100. BF2042 has 124. War Thunder….32.  Think how difficult it is to have 60 players drop on a map in Apex. My squadmates can be in Japan. A team that rolls up can have a player from Scotland and another from Kansas.  Ping may be an issue, but there is no geolock. Each player feels like they are in the same world. Each player can interact with the other.   There is a massive mountain that needs to be scaled for the game we all want to be brought to life.Forget the latency problem, or the gpu/cpu problem—> the server problem is the one I obsess on the most.  Why? Because it won’t feel immersive if a fleet battle is capped at 50 players per server.  This alpha is the one that has meant the most to me so far. I’ve bugged them so much about another project because I feel it is the single most important roadblock for any web3 game to overcome.  Eve is lauded for huge battles…thousands of players in one instance. Except the servers are melted. Slowed to a crawl.  To bring the possibility of even 1000 players per instance with the fidelity of UE5 to market ? Just the possibility of this existing in 5 years? It’s sublime. It’s simply beautiful.  And if the world is persistent? Then all bets are off…no way to imagine the impact of such a game.  1000 players battle for an asteroid. Your ship crashes on the asteroid. The battle lasts for hours. And days later a salvage ship discovers your wreck and begins to salvage for profit. Or…you drop your sidearm at a space station….and someone picks it up days later.  The team continues to allow me to dream that we have a chance for all of this. I don’t care if it’s at a smaller scale at first. I don’t care there’s only a 10% chance. That 10% contains a mirror world based in the far future. That 10% has an infinite horizon.

author: [AEP] Siphaawal
content: Well….I think Showroom is make or break for the project.  Ships are just pictures…”Jpeg NFTs” when we can walk around and perhaps inside these ships their scale will light up our lizard brains. We will let our imagination soar as we begin to feel the ships are real…..  And we own them.  This will be like our hangar module era in SC.  And it will work hand in hand with scream. We will project our feelings—our love—for our ships into the gameplay of Scream…  The same way a player projects a mental image of their rare skin when playing a first person shooter. They can’t see it, but it still is valuable to them.  Or Showroom flops and nobody cares. Who knows. We shall see.

author: [EMP] jith blade
content: Hi! trying to understand why showroom is make or break?  I do acknowledge your point of some people may find it as a bonding experience with their ships. However, I believe ( and hope ) this is a small percentage of the population.   My understanding is that showroom is a just a place to test all assets and make them ready for the UE5 game.  i.e you and I go in there look around our ships point out flaws in look and other mechanics ( doors, may be flight etc ).  Show room is also where all textures are deployed first.  The only "utility" you get out of it is that you may have your friends join your instance of showroom and jump around - or may be awe at your c11. I humbly recommend lowering your expectations.   Regardless thank you for sharing your point of view. I didn't think about that.

author: ROME | wikiwikiwik
content: This is an awesome start.  A big move and much needed.  To achieve equilibrium with the existing economy, supply and demand must find balance.  Now that there has been an adjustment to supply, it would be great to see some effort toward demand.    I'd love to see Star Atlas engage in a marketing campaign to grow the community, increasing the demand for the existing supply.  What could that look like?  Focus on enrolling people into the vision of Star Atlas.  The lowest cost ship right now is $3.43 for a Fimbul Airbike.  Create some intrigue in the market without suggesting or promising any utility.  Anything under $100 is well within reach of legacy gamers. There are currently eight ships to choose from in the price range.  Fimbul Airbike $3.43 Opal Jet $4.20 Pearce X4 $5.99 Fimbul ECOS Unibomba $11.90 VSUS Solos $13.42 Fimbul Lowbie $33.79 Pearce X5 $42.50 Opal Jetjet $87.00  Create a video teaser campaign showcasing 8 entry-level ships. Sell the vision, promote the community.  Once in the community, new members will look to the larger ships as they gain an understanding (education) of what is being created.   Spitball  campaign...   “Does the ship make the player, or does the player make the ship? There are many ways to enter Star Atlas.  What’s your style?  The Star Atlas Metaverse is under construction.  Join a thriving community of early supporters and help shape the future of gaming.”

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: The variety of ships being created is amazing.  Love it.  However the optics of having a surplus of ships that are not selling does not serve the project.  Once there is gameplay and individuals have a better understanding of what ship styles suit their gameplay then there is more reason to be specific in your selection.  At this time the amount of ships and the diversity is like selecting a pair of sun glasses.  Looks cool but don't know much about their utility.  Adding more ships before there are people to buy them doesn't feel good. At least not to me.

author: odvb
content: For the average gamer, it seems like having ATLAS and POLIS currency just working in the background would make the most sense. They are taken through a process during tutorial which is tantamount to creating a crypto wallet or connecting an existing wallet, then the game somehow communicates with the wallet and processes transactions. Ideally a non-crypto gamer would never even have to interact with their wallet if they don't want to. All transactions are done in game. Probably very hard to do what I'm describing, but it seems best to just give the customer the choice. They want to play the game, let them choose how involved they want to be with the "crypto" side of it

author: Xap✨ [STAR]
content: It took me almost an hour to catch up on this chat for past few hours. I boosted the server to get access to FR to get information from devs and have meaningful discussion. Not to read that same argument over and over again by the same handful of people. Guys you are creating a very toxic environment. Giving a suggestion and option is one thing but have a non-stop debate is excruciating. Please stop. Once you have made a point please move on. If you strongly believe that SA is making a mistake and it will fail then it is in your benefit to sell and leave the game. If you are not leaving this means that you do not believe in your argument strong enough. By the way I only see about the same 5-10 people making argument for the fee structure. Almost everyone else is fine with it.

author: Xap✨ [STAR]
content: We are forgetting that this is a game. Most of the assets will be discovered and picked up in game. Hence the fee do not matter. There will be manufacturing but it will not be here for a long time. The highest tier is not easily achievable and I do not see any player going after that benefit. There is better use of your Atlas in my opinion. If you think you will "win" the game by getting a fee discount edge then you probably do not know how this game will be played.

author: odvb
content: I understand that you and a few other people don’t really appreciate the staking structure needed for lower fees but we need to take into account one of the most fundamental aspects of our future imaginary metaverse which is guilds. Obviously it would be illogical to think any average individual would be able to acquire the highest benefits with the current structure, but I think we can agree that the endgame for the projects tokenomics is replicating a real world economy, where imo no single individual is really able to enjoy much fiscal benefits unless backed by a corporation which in our context would be guilds (pooling resources together).

author: [HEIM] SDU pimpUstur
content: Imagine being annoyed after watching your financial support for a project you align with be ripped to shreds by a crypto winter, and the way to boost the morale of said investors/players is to slap a 6% fee on top of the sale of your ships without any notion of gameplay in sight. So now, as if it wasn't bad enough everybody's SA portfolio is probably down at least 70%, and to add insult to injury, we now will have to settle for 6% less should we decide to liquidate. Yeah I'm pissed, and it's even more annoying that there is literally no public info from the team about this fee structure (I don't count announcing it at a TH with 100 people attending as a public announcement). If they believed so strongly in this structure, they should be announcing it to the roof, why is that not happening? Just feels like too much important information is being withheld, seems off to me personally, as someone who has more than most invested in this project, starting to waver abit in my confidence on delivery, which was already a tall order from the beginning, but with how things are being handled is getting worse and worse. That will be my last rant on this FR for awhile, will check back again in a few days. Later all.

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: Imagine being annoyed after watching your financial support for a project you align with be ripped to shreds by a crypto winter, and the way to boost the morale of said investors/players is to slap a 6% fee on top of the sale of your ships without any notion of gameplay in sight. So now, as if it wasn't bad enough everybody's SA portfolio is probably down at least 70%, and to add insult to injury, we now will have to settle for 6% less should we decide to liquidate. Yeah I'm pissed, and it's even more annoying that there is literally no public info from the team about this fee structure (I don't count announcing it at a TH with 100 people attending as a public announcement). If they believed so strongly in this structure, they should be announcing it to the roof, why is that not happening? Just feels like too much important information is being withheld, seems off to me personally, as someone who has more than most invested in this project, starting to waver abit in my confidence on delivery, which was already a tall order from the beginning, but with how things are being handled is getting worse and worse. That will be my last rant on this FR for awhile, will check back again in a few days. Later all.

author: [EMP] jith blade
content: Yes, this is want we know so far. You receive your LP based in your effort in comparison with your faction fellows, and you receive the Atlas based on a similar calculation like in the actual DAO with the pvp. Here nothing to doubt.  But the discussion in the community was about: if more people are doing the same task you may think that they get less LP/atlas per person, right? Because everybody is doing the same and it´s harder to stand out in comparison with others.  But in the other hand, if everybody is doing the same your faction will reach faction objetives sooner, upgrade their SB sooner, and that may open a door to a more profitable missions or tasks and you could make more Atlas in that case.  This is mainly a question about if it´s more profitable being in the faction with more members or being in the faction with less members.

author: Support Line | Star Atlas Mod [STAR]
content: If more people are doing the same, they will earn less ATLAS per person (though LP received stays the same).  You will reach objectives faster, perhaps, but there are an unlimited amount of objectives. Even if you would claim your entire MRZ and have starbases everywhere, there are two other zones you can dive into and attack. And your zone will get attacked as well.  If you would have conquered half the MRZ of both other factions, you will have spread yourself so thin, that you are bound to lose a lot of them again. It auto-balances   And new people coming in will want to join those other 2 factions, as they would earn double (or more) compared to joining your faction at that point. Which will also greatly help to balance this again.  In short: In SCREAM you will never be “done”.

author: Support Line | Star Atlas Mod [STAR]
content: It is this auto-balancing that concerns me.  I understand the rational (so that all factions remain balanced) but it seems to be very contrived.  Meaning which activities in particular will entitle you to the atlas emissions.  Surely, it's not just joining a weaker faction.  In FR people also said it's not raiding.  So I'm assuming, it's mining/transport/processing/etc...  So basically, SA is determining the market prices of these activities plus adding a premium for weaker factions.  My issues:  (1). this looks like it's heading to a centrally-planned economy where SA determines prices.  (2) This will lead to very limited 3rd party innovation because SA determines the price and can change it as they see fit.  (3) different prices for the same thing leads to unintended consequences--which could be a nothing burger because people can just create a new account and move ships across factions if they want to game it, (4) which just makes it so contrived and removes a layer of a believable meta-verse for me.   I may be misreading this auto-balancing of atlas distribution so glad to be corrected.

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content:
Owning more starbases than other factions is what it is about. I'm not sure if that would constitute a "higher level of civilization", but let's not go there 

The balancing mechanism is there for a reason. For most people it won't be profitable to move their assets around from account to account. New players are going to flock to the factions that underrepresented. There will be a very clear LP to ATLAS dashboard (if not made by the team, then by the community) that will clearly point out which faction will earn you the most.

And with "new players" I also mean returning players that are not here day 1 of SCREAM. I foresee wild swings in faction earnings during the initial period myself, but we'll have to wait & see

I may be misreading this auto-balancing of atlas distribution so glad to be corrected.

author: [Dark] gtl3Ustur
content: To be clear, there are not diminishing returns on taking the same action as another player. However, intrinsically, if everyone is exclusively participating in the highest XP/LP activities, it will lead to the creation of a larger amount of LP in a given period, and therefore a lower proportional share for each player. However, this dynamic really cannot exist, as a military cannot continue without the resources required to operate. So, either someone would shift to resource production, which would likely have a significant margin given supply/demand issues, or players would need to shift to horizontal integration of their operations to produce the resources necessary (both have the same net effect). In the former, it might well be more profitable to produce all of the resources for the war, without actually engaging in the higher XP/LP activity, because it would be safer and more profitable.  1. There should be every expectation that we will be tightly managing the economy at this early stage. Not because we want to, but out of necessity. We can't control prices of anything, though we can manage the levers behind activity and rewards.  2. Greater innovation opportunities unlock over time as the ecosystem stabilizes.  3. Faction switching comes at a cost. That player would lose access to their account progression, which is the gating mechanism enabling them to utilize more of their assets.  4. This is a process. Won't all be fully functional and balanced on day 1. Eyes on the long term prize.

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content:
Alright, let me try to explain:

 LP is account bound, you can spend it in the internal shop on hangars & undisclosed game-related items

 How ATLAS emissions work:
 At the start of each epoch, an internal counter is reset that keeps track of LP earned per player
 You earn LP for taking faction mandated actions. When you do this the counter increases with the amount of LP earned
 At the end of each epoch that internal counter is checked and your ATLAS reward is being calculated based on your LP earned that epoch vs all LP earned that epoch in your faction

 This means you can absolutely spend the LP you earn right away, without influencing the amount of ATLAS you'll receive at the end an epoch

author: [AEP]Rhododendron
content: It may be better to just, "autopay" funds due to players, in as many cases as possible.  One reason for this, is that when player funds are sitting "somewhere" not yet claimed by the player, but still in existence "somewhere" ... when this is the case x several or many thousands of players, ... there is a significant amount of currency sitting "somewhere", and there is the temptation for a human being with the ability to do so, to "temporarily" invest these unclaimed funds, which may or may not work .... until it doesn't work.  If leverage was involved in this "investment activity", regardless of what level or not of authorization, leverage can be involved ... to 100x for example ( more still when the leveraged assets are  themselves, further leveraged), and become a problem for the continued existence of the project.  I hope pools of currency are not left " somewhere " , until they are claimed.  This is a risk.  Why choose to take this risk.  Just autopay the funds.  My two satoshis.

author: 𝕘𝕣𝕚𝕤𝕥
content:
I don’t want to allow too much hope about Epic. But if this is announced SA could be part of the multiple events that brings NFTs to the masses.

The madness that is Reddit NFTs blew me away. Someone sold a free mint (Singularity #23)for $480 on Opensea. A mouths sold for .69 ETH yesterday. The floor for the collection is .28 ETH. These are $10,$50 and $75 mints.

We then still have Dr Disrespect’s project to release….with a 20k avg viewer streamer part of a blockchain game.

And a possible Epic store spot for SA? That will just add to the adoption of the Revolution. In a few years not owning your digital assets will seem ridiculous . And SA will be at the tip of the spear .

author: [AEP] Mandalorian (Djin Djarin)
content: You can generate a wallet within the UE client, which is encrypted by an address-specific password, and only stored locally on your machine. Arguably safer than using any web-based wallet (as we experienced in the Slope incident). If you want to see a ship in-engine, you can move it to your UE wallet, play around, then send back to cold storage when you're done.   I won't advise specifically to do this, as there is a chance your machine is already compromised with a backdoor/vulnerability, in which case your assets could be stolen. You can determine what level of risk exists for yourself and make that determination. But that is just one alternative.  You could also generate a new seed phrase using a web wallet and import that seed phrase into UE/Showroom. Again, varying levels of risk associated with that approach.   We are working hard to get Ledger support finalized.  edit: Updated "wallet-specific" to "address-specific"

reply.preview: You can generate a wallet within the UE client, which is encrypted by an address-specific password, and only stored locally on your machine. Arguably safer than using any web-based wallet (as we experienced in the Slope incident). If you want to see a ship in-engine, you can move it to your UE wallet, play around, then send back to cold storage when you're done. I won't advise specifically to do this, as there is a chance your machine is already compromised with a backdoor/vulnerability, in which case your assets could be stolen. You can determine what level of risk exists for yourself and make that determination. But that is just one alternative. You could also generate a new seed phrase using a web wallet and import that seed phrase into UE/Showroom. Again, varying levels of risk associated with that approach. We are working hard to get Ledger support finalized. edit: Updated "wallet-specific" to "address-specific"

author: CheckmateONI
content:
i dont see the necessity of making so complicated. i imagine few if anyone in the community would have a problem with all ships being visible in the showroom, not only those that they currently own. as Ledger is not supported in R1, which makes sense due to the complexity of the integration, this would prompt users to take an unnecessary risk by transferring ships to a hot wallet. 

the most simple solution would be to grant access to all ships for everyone in R1, and as soon as Ledger support is enabled (potentially in R2) you can still return to NFT ownership utility. it would eliminate unnecessary risk-taking on one side and enhance accessibility not only in this community, but those that will inevitable become interested in Star Atlas. I imagine the effect of being able to see all ships from scratch, even if you dont own any will generate the desire to buy some ships at the marketplace. it cannot be in the interest of the Star Atlas Team that members who currently hold their ships on safe Ledgers transfer them to unsafe hot wallets just to be able to enjoy the experience we have waited so long for to manifest

author: SW4GN3R [STAR]
content: Yes, this is exactly right. Given the depth of blockchain integration into every facet of Star Atlas, it is important to set the precedent today. A significant degree of game logic utilizes Solana as a backend game server in our case (e.g. R4 resource management within SCORE), which implies that tx signing is going to be an integral part of your day-to-day life inside the game. I'll only briefly caveat to note that we are working diligently on improving that experience so it is smooth, seamless, safe, and convenient (signing your ledger every 5 minutes would be a pain - and won't be necessary in the future). But we're building today for the vision of tomorrow.

Also Amd will announce their RX 7000 series Nov 3. 

The new 4000 cards have an interesting feature called “Remix” it’s a way to introduce Nvidia’s Omniverse…their web 3 play.

author: SpriteVonPixel [RR]
content: I've actually got a proposal that came up from exploring via this AI image generation. The idea is that we have a water based planet where we can park our ships. Just tons of ship floating on the water surface. no need for landing pads. we can scoot around on smaller ships and visit each others larger floating spaceships. Like a hub for people to park and socialize. Create docks for communal social and trade areas. Sure we can do it in space but a water planet would ad nice even dimension to it and central place where we can congregate.

author: [AEP] Bostarch
content: Yes, this is exactly right. Given the depth of blockchain integration into every facet of Star Atlas, it is important to set the precedent today. A significant degree of game logic utilizes Solana as a backend game server in our case (e.g. R4 resource management within SCORE), which implies that tx signing is going to be an integral part of your day-to-day life inside the game. I'll only briefly caveat to note that we are working diligently on improving that experience so it is smooth, seamless, safe, and convenient (signing your ledger every 5 minutes would be a pain - and won't be necessary in the future). But we're building today for the vision of tomorrow.

reply.preview: Yes, this is exactly right. Given the depth of blockchain integration into every facet of Star Atlas, it is important to set the precedent today. A significant degree of game logic utilizes Solana as a backend game server in our case (e.g. R4 resource management within SCORE), which implies that tx signing is going to be an integral part of your day-to-day life inside the game. I'll only briefly caveat to note that we are working diligently on improving that experience so it is smooth, seamless, safe, and convenient (signing your ledger every 5 minutes would be a pain - and won't be necessary in the future). But we're building today for the vision of tomorrow.

author: CLUB | Loid
content: One current example is that you can use factory to delegate a hot wallet to resupply a cold wallets ships locked in SCORE (or any wallet for that matter). Because the resupply function itself doesn't require the actual owner of the fleet to be the one to do the resupply. So, for example if you were a guild, you could offer free resupply as a perk of membership.
